# AGENTS.md â€” Rivet Project Instructions

> This section was generated by `rivet init --agents`. Re-run the command
> any time artifacts change to keep it current.

## Project Overview

This project uses **Rivet** for SDLC artifact traceability.
- Config: `rivet.yaml`
- Schemas: common, dev
- Artifacts: 2 across 2 types
- Validation: `rivet validate` (current status: pass)

## Available Commands

| Command | Purpose | Example |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `rivet validate` | Check link integrity, coverage, required fields | `rivet validate --format json` |
| `rivet list` | List artifacts with filters | `rivet list --type requirement --format json` |
| `rivet stats` | Show artifact counts by type | `rivet stats --format json` |
| `rivet add` | Create a new artifact | `rivet add -t requirement --title "..." --link "satisfies:SC-1"` |
| `rivet link` | Add a link between artifacts | `rivet link SOURCE -t satisfies --target TARGET` |
| `rivet serve` | Start the dashboard | `rivet serve --port 3000` |
| `rivet export` | Generate HTML reports | `rivet export --format html --output ./dist` |
| `rivet impact` | Show change impact | `rivet impact --since HEAD~1` |
| `rivet coverage` | Show traceability coverage | `rivet coverage --format json` |
| `rivet diff` | Compare artifact versions | `rivet diff --base path/old --head path/new` |

## Artifact Types

| Type | Count | Description |
|------|------:|-------------|
| `feature` | 1 | A user-visible capability or feature |
| `requirement` | 1 | A functional or non-functional requirement |
| `design-decision` | 0 | An architectural or design decision with rationale |

## Working with Artifacts

### File Structure
- Artifacts are stored as YAML files in: `artifacts`
- Schema definitions: `schemas/` directory
- Documents: (none configured)

### Creating Artifacts
```bash
rivet add -t requirement --title "New requirement" --status draft --link "satisfies:SC-1"
```

### Validating Changes
Always run `rivet validate` after modifying artifact YAML files.
Use `rivet validate --format json` for machine-readable output.

### Link Types

| Link Type | Description | Inverse |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| `allocated-to` | Source is allocated to the target (e.g. requirement to architecture component) | `allocated-from` |
| `constrained-by` | Source is constrained by the target | `constrains` |
| `depends-on` | Source depends on target being completed first | `depended-on-by` |
| `derives-from` | Source is derived from the target | `derived-into` |
| `implements` | Source implements the target | `implemented-by` |
| `mitigates` | Source mitigates or prevents the target | `mitigated-by` |
| `refines` | Source is a refinement or decomposition of the target | `refined-by` |
| `satisfies` | Source satisfies or fulfils the target | `satisfied-by` |
| `traces-to` | General traceability link between any two artifacts | `traced-from` |
| `verifies` | Source verifies or validates the target | `verified-by` |

## Conventions

- Artifact IDs follow the pattern: PREFIX-NNN (e.g., REQ-001, FEAT-042)
- Use `rivet add` to create artifacts (auto-generates next ID)
- Always include traceability links when creating artifacts
- Run `rivet validate` before committing

# Project Guidance (Manually Maintained)

This section is **not** managed by rivet. Edit freely. It captures the
project-specific context, argument, and discipline that a fresh agent needs
to work on witness effectively.

## What witness is

witness is a small CLI tool that produces MC/DC-style branch coverage reports
for WebAssembly components. Point it at a `.wasm` module and a test-harness
command; get a coverage report keyed to `(module, function, offset)` out.

## Why witness exists

The tool is the empirical backstop for an argument made across three blog
posts in the PulseEngine arc:

1. **[Spec-driven development is half the loop](https://pulseengine.eu/blog/spec-driven-development-is-half-the-loop/)** (shipped) â€” oracle-gated agents downstream of the spec, mechanical instruments at every verification layer, MBSE driving the build.
2. **MC/DC for AI-authored Rust is tractable â€” the variant-pruning argument** (planned, pulseengine.eu `content/blog/2026-04-24-variant-pruning-rust-mcdc.md`) â€” five layers of variant pruning (requirements â†’ cargo features â†’ cfg â†’ type system â†’ match arms) collapse the MC/DC burden to what one shipped variant actually exposes. Wasm extends the five-layer model: once the module exists, pattern matching has already lowered to `br_if` / `br_table`, cfg has elided dead code, and type-state has resolved.
3. **witness announcement** (planned, pulseengine.eu `content/blog/2026-04-25-witness-wasm-mcdc.md`) â€” the tool that turns the argument from prose to measurement.

The core tracking issue is [pulseengine.eu #29](https://github.com/pulseengine/pulseengine.eu/issues/29).

The parent post of the blog arc â€” *[Overdoing the verification chain](https://pulseengine.eu/blog/overdoing-the-verification-chain/)* (draft) â€” is the
layering thesis witness must honour. See `docs/research/overdo-alignment.md`
for the alignment brief (C1â€“C7 design constraints extracted from that post).

### The C-macro precedent

MC/DC for C is measured post-preprocessor, not on pre-preprocessor source.
Nobody argues this is illegitimate; DO-178C has accepted it since 1992.
witness measures post-rustc Wasm, which is the structural analog. If
post-preprocessor C is accepted, post-rustc Wasm is accepted. Plus the Wasm
instruction set has a machine-readable spec and a reference test suite that
C's language standard does not.

### The overdo stance

When Ferrous/DLR Rust-level MC/DC tooling ships under the 2026 Rust Project
Goal, **witness does not become obsolete**. Different measurement points
have different blind spots; translation validation bridges them; coverage at
both levels is additive evidence. We adopt both. Resistance is futile. This
is the overdo principle from *Overdoing the verification chain*.

## Scope discipline â€” v0.1 is deliberately small

The roadmap (`DESIGN.md`) runs v0.1 â†’ v1.0. Do not mix versions:

| Version | Ships |
|---|---|
| v0.1 (shipped 2026-04-24) | Strict per-`br_if` / per-`if-else` branch coverage and "executed" counting for `br_table`. Instrument, run, report. Counter values are exposed via exported mutable globals (`__witness_counter_<id>`), not via a dump function â€” so any runtime that can read Wasm globals can extract coverage without the module-under-test cooperating. Run-phase uses embedded wasmtime (subprocess-harness is v0.2). No MC/DC condition decomposition yet. No rivet integration. No sigil predicate emission. |
| v0.2 | DWARF-informed condition decomposition with strict per-`br_if` fallback when DWARF is absent. Per-target `br_table` counting (DWARF-driven). **No artificial condition-count cap** (witness has no encoder constraint, unlike LLVM/rustc which cap at 6). Subprocess `--harness <cmd>` mode as embedded-runtime escape hatch. Coverage-lifting writeup: short paper proving the reconstruction algorithm sound and the resulting coverage-lift valid relative to source-level MC/DC. |
| v0.3 | rivet evidence emit (`witness rivet-evidence`, schema `https://pulseengine.eu/witness-rivet-evidence/v1`) consumed by the upstreamed `rivet-core::coverage_evidence::CoverageStore`. In-toto coverage predicate emit (`witness predicate`, type `https://pulseengine.eu/witness-coverage/v1`) consumed opaquely by sigil's existing DSSE bundle reader. `witness merge` for cross-run aggregation. Quality bar: proptest, cargo-mutants, miri, 75% coverage threshold. |
| v0.4 | DWARF-grounded MC/DC reconstruction algorithm body (decisions::reconstruct_decisions parses Wasm DWARF custom sections via gimli, groups br_if branches sharing source-line into Decisions). `witness diff` subcommand. `witness-delta.yml` PR workflow. `actions/compliance` composite action emitting release-time evidence bundles. Mythos slop-hunt applied. Compiler-qualification reduction research published. |
| v0.5 | Workspace split: `witness-core` library compiles to wasm32-wasip2; `witness` binary keeps the wasmtime runner. New `witness lcov` subcommand emits LCOV with DWARF-correlated BRDA records and a sibling overview text for uncorrelated branches. New `witness attest` subcommand produces DSSE-signed envelopes via `wsc-attestation` (path-dep on sigil's crate; Ed25519 signing). CI dogfood loop instruments the fixture and uploads bytecode coverage to codecov as `flag: wasm-bytecode` alongside `flag: rust-source`. witness-core Wasm artefact attached to releases. Loom + meld upstream issue drafts captured for v0.6. |
| v0.6 | DWARF preservation through loom optimisation + meld fusion (gated on upstream issues). Component-model coverage with WIT interface. Post-loom / post-meld measurement points. |
| v1.0 | Check-It qualification artifact â€” emit a checkable attestation; qualify the checker, not the emitter. |

If a v0.2 concern surfaces during v0.1 work, file it as a rivet artifact or
a `TODO` comment referencing the version. Do not yak-shave forward; the
cost is too high and the v0.1 evidence is already useful on its own.

## Ecosystem relationships

| Tool | How witness touches it |
|---|---|
| **[rivet](https://github.com/pulseengine/rivet)** | Consumes witness reports as requirement-to-test evidence (v0.3). rivet.yaml + artifacts/ in this repo use rivet's dev preset. |
| **[sigil](https://github.com/pulseengine/sigil)** | Carries witness reports as in-toto coverage predicates in signed attestation bundles (v0.3). The draft SDD post has the correction on what sigil actually does today vs. what's in the pipe. |
| **[loom](https://github.com/pulseengine/loom)** | Emits post-optimization Wasm with Z3 translation validation. witness can measure coverage on loom's output (v0.4). |
| **[meld](https://github.com/pulseengine/meld)** | Component fusion. witness can measure fused components or individual components before fusion (v0.4). |
| **[kiln](https://github.com/pulseengine/kiln)** | Wasm interpreter / runtime â€” one valid execution target for the test harness. witness is runtime-agnostic in principle. |
| **[spar](https://github.com/pulseengine/spar)** | MBSE / architecture. Not directly in the coverage path, but the variant spar + rivet select determines which Wasm gets produced, which determines what witness measures. |

## The hard open research question

**Decision granularity at the Wasm level.** Short-circuit evaluation at Rust
source (`a && b && c`) compiles to a sequence of `br_if` instructions. MC/DC
requires that each condition independently affects the decision outcome.
Two honest interpretations:

- **Strict** â€” each `br_if` is its own decision. v0.1 uses this. Simple to
  implement, simple to qualify, loses the source-level grouping.
- **Reconstructed** â€” group the `br_if` sequence back into the source-level
  decision using DWARF-in-Wasm or compiler hints. v0.2's task. The grouping
  algorithm plus its soundness relative to source-level MC/DC is worth a
  short paper.

The MC/DC-on-bytecode research brief (`docs/research/mcdc-bytecode-research.md`)
established three constraints for v0.2 that should not regress:

1. **No condition-count cap.** Clang and rustc both cap MC/DC at 6
   conditions per decision because of LLVM's bitmap encoding. Witness
   has no encoder constraint and must support unbounded decisions.
2. **Per-target `br_table` counting belongs in v0.2 alongside DWARF
   reconstruction.** DWARF maps `br_table` targets to source-level match
   arms; splitting these across versions leaves Rust pattern-matching
   coverage half-done.
3. **Coverage-lifting is an explicit deliverable.** Translation-validation
   literature has the formal apparatus, but no prior work formalises
   *coverage* lifting. The v0.2 paper makes the soundness argument:
   "Wasm-level decision X covers source-level decision Y because the
   lifting from low-level to source-level is sound under DWARF-grounded
   reconstruction."

See `DESIGN.md` Â§"v0.2 â€” MC/DC at Wasm level" for the full treatment.

## Invariants that must not regress

1. **Semantic preservation under instrumentation.** For any well-formed
   input `I`, the instrumented module produces the same observable output
   as the original, modulo the exported `__witness_counter_*` globals
   and (in v0.2+) the `__witness_brtable_*` helper functions. Verify
   with round-trip tests before merging any instrumentation change.
2. **Branch IDs map to original-module offsets, not instrumented-module
   offsets.** The manifest must reference the pre-instrumentation CFG.
3. **Reports are deterministic.** Same `(module, run-data)` â†’ same report.
   No HashMap iteration order leaking into output.
4. **The manifest format is stable within a major version.** Breaking
   changes bump the major version.

## Working rules for agents

- **Before committing**, run `cargo test` and `rivet validate`. Both are
  enforced by the pre-commit hook installed by `rivet init --hooks`.
- **Commit messages** use Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`,
  `chore:`, `refactor:`, `test:`). When the commit implements or verifies
  a rivet artifact, add a trailer: `Implements: REQ-NNN` or
  `Verifies: REQ-NNN`.
- **New requirements go in `artifacts/requirements.yaml` with `status:
  draft`.** Move to `status: approved` only after human review.
- **Read `DESIGN.md` before touching instrumentation logic.** The
  semantic-preservation invariant is easy to break and hard to recover.
- **If you find yourself implementing something that looks like v0.2+
  work during v0.1**, stop. File a rivet artifact or a TODO comment and
  get back to v0.1 scope.

## Repository conventions

- **Branch protection**: `main` is protected; use feature branches + PRs.
- **License**: dual Apache-2.0 OR MIT.
- **Rust edition**: 2024 (MSRV 1.85).
- **No emojis in code or commits unless explicitly requested.**
